sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
1.1 This Standard applies to quinoa ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd.) processed as defined in Section 2.2, suitable for human consumption, packaged or in bulk.  
1.2 It does not apply to quinoa used as seeds for propagation, products derived from quinoa (e.g., flour, flakes).

sections.title: Processed quinoa
sections.text: Processed quinoa are quinoa grain ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd) that have been subjected to cleaning, removing saponin-containing pericarp and sorting (by color and size).
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Processed quinoa
number=2.2
level=2

page_spans.text: Processed quinoa are quinoa grain ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd) that have been subjected to cleaning, removing saponin-containing pericarp and sorting (by color and size).

sections.title: Quality factors - general
sections.text:
3.1.1 Quinoa shall be safe and suitable for human consumption. 
3.1.2 Quinoa shall be free from abnormal flavours and odours.  
3.1.3 Quinoa shall be free from living insects and mites.  
3.1.4 Processed Quinoa color should be characteristic, where the most common are white (pearly, pale, grayish), black and red, among others.

sections.title: The packaging shall be safe and suitable for the intended use and shall not transfer toxic materials, odours or flavours to the product. sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.

sections.title: Name of the product
sections.text: The product name appearing on the label shall be “quinoa” or “processed quinoa” , consistent with the descriptions in Section 2 of this Standard. Optional information, such as product origin, quality, color, may be included.

sections.title: Non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non-retail containers shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sentences.original:
1.1 This Standard applies to quinoa ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd.) processed as defined in Section 2.2, suitable for human consumption, packaged or in bulk.  
1.2

classification.sentence: It does not apply to quinoa used as seeds for propagation, products derived from quinoa (e.g., flour, flakes).
classification.pattern: Negation of applicability followed by enumeration of exceptions with examples in parentheses

Entities excluded: quinoa used as seeds for propagation
products derived from quinoa (e.g., flour, flakes)

classification.justification: The sentence structurally expresses a negation of applicability specifying exceptions by enumerating excluded entities, which does not match known templates but clearly conveys a prohibition modality with exception function.
notes: The sentence uses negation of applicability rather than direct prohibition, which is a less common pattern.
Enumeration with examples is explicitly included, highlighting the exceptions.

sentences.original: It does not apply to quinoa used as seeds for propagation, products derived from quinoa (e.g., flour, flakes).

classification.pattern: Negation of applicability followed by enumeration of exceptions with examples in parentheses

products derived from quinoa (e.g., flour, flakes)

classification.justification: The sentence structurally expresses a negation of applicability specifying exceptions by enumerating excluded entities, which does not match known templates but clearly conveys a prohibition modality with exception function.
notes: The sentence uses negation of applicability rather than direct prohibition, which is a less common pattern.
Enumeration with examples is explicitly included, highlighting the exceptions.

sentences.original: Quinoa is the grain obtained from Chenopodium quinoa Willd.

classification.sentence: Quinoa is the grain obtained from Chenopodium quinoa Willd.
classification.pattern: [Term] is the [class/ category] obtained from [source/species name]
classification.justification: The sentence functions as a definitional statement specifying what Quinoa is by linking the term to its biological source species. This type of definitional structure is not covered by known templates, and it is stated in indicative form rather than normative modality. The modality is closest to Condition as it sets a reference condition for the term's meaning within the regulation. Hence, a new pattern is generated reflecting definition structure.
notes: The sentence does not express obligation, permission, prohibition, or explicit quantitative limits, but rather defines a term, which is a distinct communicative function not covered by regulatory normative templates.

sentences.original: Processed quinoa are quinoa grain ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd) that have been subjected to cleaning, removing saponin-containing pericarp and sorting (by color and size).

classification.sentence: Processed quinoa are quinoa grain ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd) that have been subjected to cleaning, removing saponin-containing pericarp and sorting (by color and size).
classification.pattern: Subject + 'are' + nominal definition with embedded participial phrases detailing processes

components.definition: quinoa grain ( Chenopodium quinoa Willd) that have been subjected to cleaning, removing saponin-containing pericarp and sorting (by color and size)

classification.justification: The sentence provides a definitional statement specifying what 'Processed quinoa' consists of, including additional processing details. No normative modality or regulatory obligation is expressed. The structure corresponds to a definitional pattern, which is not present in the known template catalog, so a new pattern classification is given with modality 'undefined'.
notes: This sentence is a definition, not a normative or regulatory statement, thus classified with modality 'undefined'.

sentences.original: 3.1.1 Quinoa shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.

classification.sentence: 3.1.4 Processed Quinoa color should be characteristic, where the most common are white (pearly, pale, grayish), black and red, among others.
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + predicate + relative clause enumerating common characteristics

components.subject: Processed Quinoa color
components.relative_clause: where the most common are white (pearly, pale, grayish), black and red, among others

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation regarding the characteristic color of processed quinoa and provides specific descriptive details in a relative clause. This structure and communicative function do not closely match any known templates.
notes: The sentence combines a normative statement of quality with an enumerated descriptive list, which is uncommon in standard templates.

sentences.original: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.

classification.sentence: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.
classification.pattern: Subject (Lower moisture limits) + Modal verb (should) + Passive verb phrase (be required) + Purpose/Condition clause (for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage)

components.subject: Lower moisture limits
components.condition: for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation to require lower moisture limits, structured with a subject, modal verb 'should' indicating recommendation or obligation, and a conditional purpose specifying destinations related to climate and transport factors. No existing template strongly matches this nuanced obligation tied to environmental and logistical conditions, thus a new pattern is assigned.
notes: The use of 'should' reflects a soft obligation or recommendation rather than a strict mandate, which may overlap with obligation and permission categories in some frameworks.

sentences.original: The use of food additives is not permitted.

classification.sentence: The use of food additives is not permitted.
classification.pattern: Subject (the use of [entity]) + copula + negated permission predicate (is not permitted)
classification.justification: The sentence expresses a prohibition modality, specifically forbidding the use of food additives. Although it fits the general sense of prohibition, it does not match any known templates and the structure with 'is not permitted' is a negated permission form rather than a direct prohibition phrasing like 'shall not'. Hence, the template match score is low and a new pattern is identified with a prohibition modality and forbidding communicative function.
notes: The sentence uses a negation of permission modality ('is not permitted'), which overlaps conceptually with prohibition but is structurally distinct from typical prohibition templates.
No exact or close template found in the current catalog; structural pattern is inferred.

sentences.original: The product covered by this Standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

classification.sentence: The product covered by this Standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
classification.pattern: [Subject] shall be labelled in accordance with [Reference Standard]

components.Subject: The product covered by this Standard
components.Reference Standard: the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes a mandatory labelling requirement referencing another standard, with a clear obligation modality and prescriptive function. No known template matches precisely, so a new pattern reflecting the obligation to label in accordance with another standard is inferred.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR QUINOA
pages.summary: Página apresenta título principal do documento e cabeçalho com código e ano, sem seções estruturadas nem texto adicional.

Página contém 3 seções principais (1. SCOPE, 2. DESCRIPTION, 3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS) com múltiplos níveis de subseções numeradas hierarquicamente conforme o padrão, texto explicativo associado, e continuidade sinalizada na seção 3.2.3.1 e filhos, que seguem para próxima página.

Página contém seções de níveis 1 a 3, com títulos numerados e em caixa alta, texto associado claro, e continuidade indicada em 3.2.4 que continua da página anterior. A estrutura hierárquica é coerente com a numeração padrão de seções do documento.

Página com seções principais 8 e 9. Seção 8 indicada como agrupadora maior sem título visual, contendo subseções 8.1 e 8.2 com texto associado respectivo. A seção 8 parece continuar na próxima página, conforme ausência de título final na página. Seção 9 inicia com título destacado nível 1, contendo texto explicativo direto. Não há indicação de continuidade desde a página anterior para as seções nesta página.
